lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.actual_rsi_2_trade_density: Parallel s13-d1 P6 IS produced 159 closed_trades over 4.6297y = ~34 trades/year. This is BELOW the K9-reachability PLAN/SEAL lower bound of 46/y, but ABOVE the K9 threshold (159 > 100 over IS window).
lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.arithmetic_lesson: For DR10 threshold 0.50 with $200k equity, max acceptable annual dollar-turnover is $100k/year. With per-trade notional ~$75k (typical for MNQ.c.0 at 0.5pct risk), this caps max trades-per-year at ~1.33. K9 requires >=22 trades/year over 4.6y IS. The 22 vs 1.33 trades-per-year gap is the K9/DR10 structural tension exposed by this candidate.
lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.future_design_implication: Future candidates seeking K9 clearance on single futures instruments must either: (a) accept DR10 threshold elevation at SEAL via DA19=B (turnover threshold 1.00 or higher); (b) drastically reduce per-trade notional (e.g., 0.1pct risk, single-contract sub-fractional sizing); (c) use a multi-instrument universe to spread turnover across more equity; (d) pivot to a different asset class (e.g., cash equities with lower per-trade notional).
lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.higher_frequency_pivot_to_rsi_2: s13-d1 / T1 pivoted mechanic family from F1 (Donchian trend) to F3 (RSI mean-reversion), specifically Connors RSI(2) bi-directional. K9-reachability discipline at PLAN/SEAL conjectured 46-68 IS trades/year (210-313 over 4.6y).
lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.k9_problem_solved: IS K9 clears with margin (1.59x threshold). OOS K9 at proportional scaling (34/y x 2.0y = 68.7) BELOW threshold of 100 (per alignment review revision). Per DA20=A disposition is DR1 INCONCLUSIVE_HOLD; not REJECT_FAST.
lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.load_bearing_lesson: For single-instrument strategies on multi-year windows, K9 and DR10 thresholds form a structural tension. Solving K9 by increasing signal density (faster mechanic) typically pushes turnover ratio above DR10 threshold. The two thresholds need to be jointly satisfiable: either DR10 threshold raised at SEAL (DA19=B at T1 DRAFT was offered; not chosen), or mechanic chosen with structurally just-sufficient signal density (above K9, below DR10).
lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.sample_size_problem_recap: s12-d1 (Donchian-15/8 MNQ.c.0) parked at P6 IS with INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE (33 closed_trades < K9 threshold of 100). Donchian-15/8 was conjectured at PLAN to produce 80-200 IS trades; actual was 33 (well below P2 lower bound).
lesson_higher_frequency_rsi_solved_sampl.turnover_explosion_problem_created: Same signal density that solved K9 drove annual_turnover to 84.7851 - 169x over DR10 threshold of 0.50. DR10 RF1 fires REJECT_FAST regardless of edge survival (DR3 does not fire; all 4 cost tiers retain positive expectancy + net PnL).

why_rsi_2_solved_k9_but_failed_dr10.load_bearing_lesson: K9 and DR10 form a structural tension. K9 demands sufficient samples (>=100 closed_trades per window); DR10 demands turnover ratio (annual dollar-turnover/equity) below 0.50. For single-instrument strategies on a 4.6y IS window, clearing K9 typically requires >=22 trades/year (= 100/4.6); clearing DR10 typically requires position size kept small enough that 22 trades x per_trade_notional / equity < 0.50. On MNQ.c.0 with 0.5pct risk on $200k cash, per-trade notional is ~$50-100k (depends on ATR). 22 trades x $75k / $200k = ~8 annual turnover ratio = 16x over threshold. The arithmetic shows DR10 is structurally hard to clear at K9-feasible signal density on single futures instruments at typical sizing. Either: (a) raise DR10 threshold structurally (DA19=B alternative at SEAL; offered at T1 DRAFT not chosen at SEAL); or (b) reduce per-trade notional drastically (sub-1pct of equity); or (c) pivot to lower-frequency mechanic so trades-per-year is just barely above K9 threshold.
why_rsi_2_solved_k9_but_failed_dr10.why_higher_frequency_solved_one_problem_: RSI(2) bi-directional mean-reversion on MNQ.c.0 produces ~34 trades/year on the 4.6y IS window (159 over 4.6297y), which is 4-5x more than s12-d1's Donchian-15/8 mechanic (~7-10/y). This higher signal frequency CLEARED IS K9 (159 > 100 threshold). However, the same higher frequency drove annual_turnover to 84.7851 - 169x over the DR10 threshold of 0.50. The structural tension is: K9 demands MORE trades (above 100 over IS + OOS); DR10 demands LESS turnover (below 0.50 annual). These constraints are in tension for any per-trade-sized strategy on a single instrument over a finite history window. Solving K9 by increasing signal frequency necessarily increases turnover; if turnover exceeds DR10 threshold, the candidate is rejected on cost-stress regardless of edge survival.
